title: Sustained E-commerce Card Drain via Low-Amount Digital Goods Purchases
narrative: Fraudster executes 50-80 transactions over a 24-hour period targeting digital product merchants (ProductCD='W' or 'C') to drain a compromised card below fraud alert thresholds. Typical pattern shows $15-45 gift card or gaming credit purchases every 15-35 minutes, cycling through 4-6 merchant IDs to avoid single-merchant velocity blocks. Attack begins during off-hours (txn_hour 1-5 AM) when legitimate cardholder is unlikely to notice push notifications, then continues through business hours. Total drainage reaches $2,400-3,200 before card issuer blocks. Fraudster uses consistent device fingerprint and shipping address (addr1 unchanged) but rotates P_emaildomain across free providers. Card velocity metrics spike dramatically while individual transaction amounts remain innocuous.
indicators: card1_txn_count_24h between 50-80 transactions with median gap under 30 minutes
TransactionAmt consistently $15-45 with standard deviation under $8 across the series
ProductCD limited to 'W' (digital wallet loads) or 'C' (digital content) for 90%+ of transactions
4-6 distinct merchant IDs (card1_mid_count_24h) but single addr1 value throughout
Attack initiation during is_night=1 (txn_hour 1-5) followed by continuous daytime activity
P_emaildomain rotates across 3+ free providers (gmail.com, yahoo.com, outlook.com) while R_emaildomain remains NULL
card1_amt_zscore remains below 2.0 per transaction but cumulative 24h amount exceeds 15x cardholder's historical daily average
summary: A compromised card shows 50-80 small purchases ($15-45 each) for gift cards or digital content within 24 hours, starting overnight and continuing through the day with transactions every 15-35 minutes across multiple merchants but using the same shipping address.
reasoning: The fraudster keeps each transaction small to avoid triggering amount-based alerts while draining the card quickly before the legitimate owner notices, rotating email addresses and merchants to evade velocity blocks but maintaining address consistency for successful digital delivery.
indicator_explanations: Card shows relentless purchasing pace with barely any breaks between transactions
Every purchase amount clusters tightly around same low dollar range
Attacker exclusively targets easy-to-monetize digital products throughout the drain
Multiple merchants hit but billing address stays locked to single location
Attack launches in dead of night then continues nonstop through business hours
Email rotates between popular free services while recipient email stays blank
Individual charges fly under radar but daily total dwarfs cardholder's normal spending
